thinking.title: Assess thinking skills before learning programming
thinking.subtitle: This test does not assess Python or coding knowledge. It focuses on logic, patterns, conditions, algorithms, data reasoning, system thinking, and debugging mindset.
thinking.question: Question {{index}}

think001.question: Number sequence: 2, 4, 6, 8, ?. What is the next number?
think001.explain: Each number increases by 2, so the next number is 10.

think002.question: Number sequence: 2, 5, 10, 17, 26, ?. What is the next number?
think002.explain: The differences are 3, 5, 7, 9. The next difference is 11, so 26 + 11 = 37.

think003.question: Number sequence: 1, 2, 4, 7, 11, 16, ?. What is the next number?
think003.explain: The differences increase by 1: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. The next difference is 6, so 16 + 6 = 22.

think004.question: Number sequence: 3, 6, 5, 10, 9, 18, ?. What is the next number?
think004.explain: The pattern alternates: multiply by 2, then subtract 1. 3→6, 6→5, 5→10, 10→9, 9→18, so 18→17.

think005.question: All learners who complete the exercise unlock the next lesson. An completed the exercise. Which conclusion is definitely true?

options.B: An is already good at programming
options.C: An no longer needs to study

think005.explain: According to the rule, anyone who completes the exercise unlocks the next lesson.

think006.question: All A are B. Some B are C. Which conclusion is definitely true?

options.D: We cannot be certain whether A are C

think006.explain: Some B being C does not guarantee that A belongs to that part of B.

think007.question: If the system has an error, the report will be late. The report is not late. What is the most reasonable conclusion?

options.A: The system definitely has an error
options.B: The system has no error according to the given condition
options.D: We cannot know whether the report was completed

think007.explain: If there were an error, the report would be late. Since it is not late, the system has no error under this condition.

think008.question: A course requires a test score of at least 60 to enter the foundation class. Binh scores 58. Where should Binh be placed?
think008.explain: 58 is below 60, so Binh has not met the requirement for the foundation class and should take preparation first.

think009.question: A learner receives a certificate only if they complete 80% of the lessons and pass the final quiz. Mai completes 90% of the lessons but fails the quiz. Does Mai receive the certificate?
think009.explain: The rule uses 'and', so both conditions must be met. Mai failed the quiz, so she does not receive the certificate.

think010.question: Placement rule: below 40 points = thinking basics; 40-69 = foundation; 70-89 = accelerated; 90 or above = advanced. A learner scores 89. Which class should they join?
think010.explain: 89 is within the 70-89 range, so the learner belongs in the accelerated class, not advanced.

think011.question: To register an online learning account, which order is the most reasonable?

options.A: Log in → create account → enter email
options.B: Enter information → verify email → log in

think011.explain: You need to enter information, verify the email, and then log in.

think012.question: You have an unsorted list of 100 learners. To reliably find a specific name, what should you do?

options.C: Check each name one by one until found
options.D: Delete the list and re-enter it

think012.explain: When a list is unsorted, the reliable method is to check items one by one.

think013.question: You need to review 30 submissions using the same criteria. What is the most efficient approach?

options.A: Create a checklist and review each submission using it
options.B: Think of the criteria from scratch for every submission

think013.explain: A checklist reduces mistakes and saves time when repeating the same review process.

think014.question: You want to build a to-do list app. Which feature group makes the most sense?

options.A: Change screen color, play music, watch videos
options.D: Add task, edit task, mark as completed, delete task

think014.explain: A to-do list app should focus on managing tasks.

think015.question: When building a programming learning website, what should be done first?

options.A: Define learners, learning paths, lessons, and progress tracking
options.B: Choose button colors first and decide later

think015.explain: The target learners, learning structure, and progress logic should be defined before detailed UI work.

think016.question: An online learning system needs to store learning progress. Which elements are most directly related?
think016.explain: Progress tracking needs to know who is learning, which lesson they are learning, and whether it is completed.

think017.question: If lesson B only unlocks after lesson A is completed, but the learner has not completed lesson A, what should the system do?

options.C: Lock lesson B and guide the learner to complete lesson A first

think017.explain: When dependency rules exist, the later content should remain locked and the learner should be guided to complete the required lesson first.

think018.question: Revenue for 3 days is 100, 150, and 120. Which day has the highest revenue?
think018.explain: 150 is the largest number, so Day 2 has the highest revenue.

think019.question: A class has 25 people. The report says 18 passed and 10 failed. What is unusual?
think019.explain: The report totals 28 people, which exceeds the actual class size of 25.

think020.question: A learner answers 6/10 correctly on attempt 1 and 8/10 on attempt 2. Which statement is most reasonable?

options.A: The result improved by 2 correct answers

think020.explain: Going from 6 correct answers to 8 correct answers means an improvement of 2.

think021.question: A process has 5 steps but the result is wrong. What is the best way to check it?

options.C: Check each step from beginning to end

think021.explain: Checking step by step helps identify where the error appears.

think022.question: A registration form cannot be submitted. What is the most reasonable way to handle it?

options.D: Check input data, submit button, connection, and error messages

think022.explain: You should check the related parts one by one to find the real cause.

think023.question: A feature has 3 parts: input, processing, and display. The displayed result is wrong. What is the best way to check it?

options.B: Check the data after each part to locate where the error happens

think023.explain: Isolating the error by checking data through each part helps locate the actual source of the problem.

think024.question: A robot starts at the bottom-left corner of a 2x2 grid and needs to reach the top-right corner. It can only move right or up. How many shortest paths are possible?
think024.explain: The robot needs 2 right moves and 2 up moves. The number of ways to arrange 4 moves with 2 R and 2 U is 6.

placePy001.question: In programming, what is data given to a program usually called?
placePy001.explain: Input is data provided to a program for processing.

placePy002.question: In programming, what is a variable usually used for?
placePy002.explain: A variable stores data so the program can use it.

placePy003.question: If a score of 5 or above means passing, and a learner scores 6, what is the result?
placePy003.explain: 6 is greater than 5, so the passing condition is met.

groupA.title: Strong thinking skills, ready to start programming
groupA.desc: The learner shows strong pattern recognition, logical reasoning, data reasoning, and problem decomposition skills.

groupB.title: Good potential, should follow a foundation roadmap
groupB.desc: The learner can start programming but should learn slowly with visual examples and small exercises.

groupC.title: Should strengthen logical thinking before coding
groupC.desc: The learner should start with logic exercises, Scratch, and everyday algorithms before moving into Python.
